content: Drawing on the dual meaning of "reflexion" as both optical light reflection and deliberate cognitive reflection, we can frame LLM self-reflection mechanisms as an optical mirror system that multiplies distinct reflective perspectives rather than a single feedback loop. Just as a pair of facing mirrors creates infinite recursive reflections of an object to expose hidden angles unseen from a single viewpoint, a multi-perspective LLM self-reflection framework structured around this optical reflexion principle can expose more inconsistent reasoning flaws than single-perspective reflection approaches.
